description: Singles: ONE Pokemon active per side (two on the field). A match ends when all of one trainer's Pokemon have fainted.

rules.format_notes: This is SINGLES: 1 Pokemon active per side. Per Serebii's Regulation M-A page (the rules haven't changed under M-B), a Singles team is 3 to 6 Pokemon - there is NO separate team-preview 'pick 4 of 6' selection step the way Doubles has; whatever team size the player registered (3-6) is what they play with directly. Don't ask about or expect a 'brought' subset distinct from the full team the way build_event_prompt's Doubles wording does.

fields.player_active: field_state: the player's active Pokemon, if visible
fields.opponent_active: field_state: the opponent's active Pokemon, if visible

notes: This is SINGLES: ONE Pokemon active per side (two on the field). A 'field_state' lists the player's active Pokemon (player_active) and the opponent's (opponent_active), with HP in detail. A match ends only when ALL of one trainer's Pokemon have fainted, shown by a 'You won!'/defeat/results screen - emit ONE 'battle_end' per match with winner 'player' or 'opponent'. A single Pokemon fainting is 'pokemon_fainted', never 'battle_end'.
